name: 上海
description: Shanghai, China's economic powerhouse, is a dazzling metropolis where Art Deco architecture meets futuristic skyscrapers. Known as the 'Pearl of the Orient', it offers world-class dining, shopping on Nanjing Road, the historic Bund waterfront, and a vibrant nightlife scene. The city seamlessly blends Eastern and Western influences, creating a unique cosmopolitan atmosphere.
highlights: Oriental Pearl Tower - Iconic skyline landmark

climate.tips: Spring and autumn offer the most comfortable weather. Summers are hot and humid with frequent rain. Winters are cold but rarely snowy. Typhoon season is July-September. Pack layers and an umbrella.

attractions.name: 外滩
attractions.description: Shanghai's most iconic waterfront boulevard lined with 52 Gothic, Renaissance, and Art Deco buildings from the colonial era.
attractions.address: Zhongshan East One Rd, Huangpu District
attractions.tips: Best visited at sunset for golden hour photos. Walk from the Bund to Nanjing Road pedestrian street. Evening light show on the river.

attractions.name: 南京路步行街
attractions.description: The world's busiest shopping street, stretching 1.3km from People's Square to the Bund.
attractions.tips: Start at People's Square and walk to the Bund. Look for Century 21 and Shein flagship stores.

attractions.name: 豫园
attractions.description: A classical Chinese garden dating from the Ming dynasty with pavilions, halls, rockeries, and ponds.
attractions.ticketPrice: 30 CNY (garden only), 50 CNY (with City God Temple)
attractions.tips: Arrive early to avoid crowds. The surrounding Yuyuan Bazaar is great for souvenirs and local snacks.

attractions.name: 上海中心大厦
attractions.description: China's tallest building and the world's second tallest, with a 118th floor observation deck offering panoramic city views.
attractions.tips: Book online to avoid queues. Best time is sunset. Combine with visit to nearby Shanghai World Financial Center.

attractions.name: 新天地
attractions.description: A trendy entertainment district featuring restored shikumen (stone gate) houses, upscale restaurants, bars, and boutique shops.
attractions.tips: Great for evening dining. The area has both upscale and casual options. Photogenic for sunset shots.

attractions.name: 东方明珠塔
attractions.description: Shanghai's most recognizable landmark with observation decks, a rotating restaurant, and glass-bottomed skywalk.
attractions.tips: Evening tickets for best city lights. Combine with walk along the Bund for iconic skyline photos.

attractions.name: 徐家汇/法租界
attractions.description: Tree-lined streets, historic European architecture, trendy cafes, and boutiques in the former French settlement.
attractions.address: Former French Concession area, Xuhui District
attractions.tips: Best explored on foot or by bike. Visit on weekends for the Sunday brunch culture. Great area for photography.

attractions.name: 上海博物馆
attractions.description: One of China's best museums with a world-class collection of ancient Chinese art, including bronzes, ceramics, and calligraphy.
attractions.tips: Arrive early. Audio guides available. Combine with People's Square. Free entry but donation box welcomed.

attractions.name: 上海迪士尼乐园
attractions.description: China's first Disney park with the world's largest castle, innovative rides, and Chinese-themed attractions.
attractions.tips: Download the Shanghai Disneyland app. Arrive 30 min before opening. Buy Quick Pass for popular rides. Wear comfortable shoes.

attractions.name: 磁悬浮列车
attractions.description: The world's fastest commercial magnetic levitation train, reaching 431 km/h on the 30km airport link.
attractions.ticketPrice: 50 CNY (single ride), 80 CNY (round trip including metro)
attractions.tips: Worth taking just for the experience even if not going to the airport. The 8-minute ride is thrilling.

attractions.name: 上海科技馆
attractions.description: A massive interactive science museum with IMAX Dome Theater and hands-on exhibits for all ages.
attractions.tips: Great for families. Allow at least 3 hours. IMAX show highly recommended. Book tickets online.

attractions.name: 静安寺
attractions.description: One of Shanghai's most important Buddhist temples, located in the heart of the city with a golden facade.
attractions.tips: One of the few temples that charges entry. Audio guides available. Nearby Jing'an Kerry Centre for shopping.

attractions.name: 田子坊
attractions.description: A maze of narrow alleyways filled with boutique shops, art studios, cafes, and bars in a historic shikumen neighborhood.
attractions.tips: Get lost in the alleys. Best visited on weekdays. Great for finding unique souvenirs. Many shops close by 9pm.

attractions.name: 上海自然博物馆
attractions.description: A stunning modern museum housing over 30,000 specimens including dinosaur fossils, whale skeletons, and interactive exhibits.
attractions.tips: Buy tickets online in advance. Located near Shanghai Science and Technology Museum. Great for children.

attractions.name: 上海城市规划展示馆
attractions.description: A museum showcasing Shanghai's past, present, and future urban development with detailed scale models of the city.
attractions.tips: The third floor model of Shanghai is impressive. Located near People's Square metro station.

attractions.name: 上海欢乐谷
attractions.description: A large amusement park with roller coasters, water rides, and family attractions for all ages.
attractions.tips: Arrive early. Wear comfortable shoes. Single-day passport available. Take metro line 9 to Songjiang direction.

attractions.name: 中华艺术宫
attractions.description: The largest museum of art in China, housed in the former Expo 2010 China Pavilion with massive displays of Chinese art.
attractions.tips: Free entry but need to reserve online. The building itself is impressive. Combine with Expo Park walk.

attractions.name: M50创意园
attractions.description: A former factory complex turned into an art district featuring galleries, studios, and creative boutiques.
attractions.tips: Many galleries close on Mondays. Great for modern Chinese art. Nearby Suzhou Creek area has cafes.

attractions.name: 黄浦江游览
attractions.description: A scenic boat tour along the Huangpu River offering the best views of both the historic Bund and modern Pudong skyline.
attractions.address: Ferry Terminals at Bund and Pudong
attractions.tips: Book evening cruise for illuminated skyline. Bring jacket as it's cold on the water. Combine with Bund walk.

attractions.name: 1933老场坊
attractions.description: A historic slaughterhouse turned creative space with unique spiral staircases, bridges, and European architecture.
attractions.tips: The architecture is the main draw. Great for photography. Nearby Duolun Road for more art galleries.

attractions.name: 衡山路
attractions.description: A tree-lined boulevard in the former French Concession with historic villas, bars, and restaurants.
attractions.tips: Great for evening stroll. Many bars and restaurants open late. Connected to Wukang Rd area.

attractions.name: 世博会博物馆
attractions.description: The only museum in the world dedicated to the World Expo, showcasing the history and legacy of world's fairs.
attractions.tips: Free entry with reservation. Nearby Hongqiao area. Great for understanding Shanghai's development.

attractions.name: 七宝古镇
attractions.description: A historic water town with traditional architecture, street food, and crafts just 30 minutes from central Shanghai.
attractions.tips: Take Metro Line 9 to Qibao. Famous for Qibao noodles and traditional snacks. Great for a half-day trip.

attractions.name: 上海海洋水族馆
attractions.description: One of the largest aquariums in the world with over 400 species of marine life and a 155-meter underwater tunnel.
attractions.tips: Located next to Oriental Pearl Tower. Great for families. Book tickets online for discounts.

attractions.name: 外高桥自由贸易试验区
attractions.description: China's first free trade zone with international shopping centers and import goods at duty-free prices.
attractions.tips: Take Metro Line 6 to Waigaoqiao South. Good for electronics and imported goods. Bring passport.

attractions.name: 龙华古寺
attractions.description: Shanghai's largest and most authentic Buddhist temple, dating back to the Three Kingdoms period.
attractions.tips: One of Shanghai's oldest temples. Metro Line 11 to Longhua station. Beautiful at cherry blossom season.

restaurants.name: Ultraviolet by Paul Pairet
restaurants.description: Three Michelin star immersive dining experience by Chef Paul Pairet. 20 courses over 4 hours in a multi-sensory room.

restaurants.name: 唐阁
restaurants.address: New Hastings Hotel, 88 East Nanjing Rd, Huangpu
restaurants.description: Two Michelin star Cantonese restaurant with impeccable dim sum and refined Shanghai cuisine.

restaurants.name: 吉品轩
restaurants.description: Two Michelin star modern Chinese restaurant by Chef Eric Wang, showcasing creative fusion of regional cuisines.

restaurants.name: 御宝轩
restaurants.description: Three diamond Black Pearl Cantonese restaurant with exceptional dim sum and premium seafood.

restaurants.name: 上海老饭店
restaurants.description: Legendary restaurant serving authentic Shanghainese cuisine for over 40 years.
dishHighlights: Braised pork belly with brown sauce

restaurants.name: 南翔小笼
restaurants.description: Famous for authentic nanxiang xiaolongbao (steamed soup dumplings) in business since 1900.

restaurants.name: 绿波廊
restaurants.description: Historic restaurant in Yuyuan since 1940s, known for refined Shanghai snacks and Empress Dowager's favorite dishes.

restaurants.name: 光明邨大酒家
restaurants.description: Popular local spot always packed with locals for authentic Shanghai dishes and legendary fresh mooncakes.

restaurants.name: 新天地朗廷酒店唐阁
restaurants.description: Two Michelin star Cantonese restaurant with stunning Bund views and exquisite dim sum.

restaurants.name: 夜上海
restaurants.address: Waldorf Astoria Shanghai, 20 Wuchang Rd, Huangpu
restaurants.description: One Michelin star restaurant serving refined Shanghai cuisine in the historic Waldorf Astoria building.

restaurants.name: 老吉士酒家
restaurants.description: A beloved local institution famous for authentic Shanghai home-style cooking in a cozy atmosphere.

restaurants.name: 苏浙总会
restaurants.address: Bund Center, 500 Nanjing East Rd, Huangpu
restaurants.description: Three diamond Black Pearl restaurant serving refined Jiangsu and Zhejiang cuisine with excellent seafood.

restaurants.name: 大壶春
restaurants.description: Legendary spot for Shanghai's finest Shengjian bao (pan-fried pork soup dumplings) since 1937.

restaurants.name: 功德林
restaurants.description: Famous Buddhist vegetarian restaurant serving plant-based versions of traditional Shanghai dishes since 1922.

restaurants.name: 王家沙点心
restaurants.description: Popular century-old restaurant known for authentic Shanghai snacks and traditional pastries.

restaurants.name: 鲜墙房
restaurants.description: Upscale local restaurant in a historic villa serving refined Shanghai cuisine with modern touches.

restaurants.name: 外滩18号
restaurants.description: One Michelin star French restaurant in a restored colonial building with stunning Bund views.

restaurants.name: 新荣记
restaurants.description: Three diamond Black Pearl restaurant famous for high-quality ingredients and refined cooking.

restaurants.name: 上海1号私房菜
restaurants.description: Hidden gem serving home-style Shanghainese dishes in a traditional setting without pretension.

restaurants.name: 福1088
restaurants.description: Popular local restaurant in a lovely garden setting serving classic Shanghai comfort food.

restaurants.name: 失重餐厅
restaurants.description: Futuristic restaurant where food arrives via flying trays on rail system, fun for families.

restaurants.name: 荷唐·铜锣湾
restaurants.description: Popular spot serving authentic Cantonese dim sum and seafood in a modern setting.

restaurants.name: 阿娘面馆
restaurants.description: Legendary hole-in-the-wall spot for Shanghai-style large intestines stew noodles since 1970s.

restaurants.name: 上海老站
restaurants.description: Historic rotary restaurant in a converted train station serving authentic Shanghai cuisine.

restaurants.name: 福和慧
restaurants.address: 1 Y，立兴大楼, 172 Fuxing Middle Rd, Xuhui
restaurants.description: One Michelin star vegetarian restaurant offering creative plant-based fine dining.

arrival.to: Shanghai Pudong International Airport (PVG) / Hongqiao Airport (SHA)
arrival.tips: PVG handles international flights, SHA mainly domestic. Maglev train from PVG reaches 430km/h.

arrival.to: Shanghai Hongqiao Railway Station / Shanghai Railway Station
arrival.tips: High-speed trains extremely punctual. Hongqiao station connects to metro and airports.

metro: Single ride: 3-10 CNY depending on distance
taxi: Didi app preferred for non-Chinese speakers
bike: Unlock: 1 CNY, then 0.15 CNY per minute
Designated bike lanes on major roads
Electric bikes popular but require license

hotels.name: 上海半岛酒店
hotels.address: 32 The Bund, 32 Zhongshan Dong Yi Rd, Huangpu
hotels.bookingTips: Request Bund-view rooms. Best deals through hotel loyalty programs.

hotels.name: 上海外滩华尔道夫酒店
hotels.address: 2 The Bund, 20 Zhongshan Dong Yi Rd, Huangpu
highlights: Heritage building connected to new tower
hotels.bookingTips: Long Bar for cocktails is legendary. Book bar seats in advance.

hotels.name: 上海金茂君悦大酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Higher floors offer better views. Check for weekend packages.

hotels.name: 上海希尔顿酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Good central location for first-time visitors.

hotels.name: 上海艾迪逊酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Rooftop bar is popular - book ahead.

hotels.name: 上海静安香格里拉大酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Higher floors have best views. Breakfast worth the upgrade.

hotels.name: 上海镛舍
hotels.bookingTips: Connected to HKRI Taikoo Hui mall.

hotels.name: 上海宝格丽酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Bund view suites are exceptional. Book restaurant in advance.

hotels.name: 上海和平饭店
hotels.bookingTips: Request renovated rooms in the south building. Sunday jazz brunch is legendary.

hotels.name: 上海浦东香格里拉大酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Horizon Club rooms offer better views. Airport transfer recommended.

hotels.name: 上海素凯泰酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Spa treatments highly recommended. Good for business travelers.

hotels.name: 上海瑞吉酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Butler service is exceptional. Ask for renovated rooms.

hotels.name: 上海安曼酒店
hotels.address: Conrad Hotel, 500 Yincheng Middle Rd, Pudong
hotels.bookingTips: Only 28 rooms ensure exclusivity. Book well in advance.

hotels.name: 上海锦江饭店
hotels.bookingTips: Meijing building has been renovated. Great for history buffs.

hotels.name: 上海外滩悦榕庄
hotels.nameEn: Banyan Tree Shanghai On The Bund
hotels.bookingTips: Request river-view rooms. Best for romantic getaways.

hotels.name: 上海新天地安达仕酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Great for nightlife. Book weekend packages.

payment.description: Dominant payment method in Shanghai, accepted everywhere
howToUse: Bind international credit card or open local bank account
Scan merchant QR code to pay
tips: Most modern city - foreign cards work at more places
Some small vendors may only accept local cards

payment.description: Essential for local transactions and WeChat services
howToUse: Link card through WeChat Pay in settings

payment.description: Accepted but increasingly rare in Shanghai
howToUse: ICBC, HSBC, Standard Chartered have English interfaces
tips: Bank cards generally work at any ATM

payment.description: Increasingly accepted at international venues and convenience stores
howToUse: Add international card to Apple Wallet

payment.description: China's domestic card network, essential for ATM withdrawals and widely accepted
howToUse: Accepted at most merchants in Shanghai
International cards with UnionPay logo work
tips: Best for withdrawing RMB with lower fees
Widely available at all banks and ATMs
Works at supermarkets and convenience stores

payment.description: Increasingly accepted at modern venues, convenience stores, and international chains
howToUse: Works at 7-Eleven, FamilyMart, all major convenience stores
Add international card to Apple Wallet
tips: Most reliable foreign payment option after cards

culturalTips.title: Shanghainese Dialect
culturalTips.content: While Mandarin is universally understood, Shanghainese (上海话) is the local dialect. Many locals appreciate when visitors try a few phrases.

culturalTips.title: Dining Scene
culturalTips.content: Shanghai is China's culinary capital with incredible diversity. Reservations essential for popular restaurants. Peak dining is 7-8pm.

culturalTips.title: Dress Code
culturalTips.content: Shanghai is fashion-forward. Smart casual is standard for restaurants and bars. Some upscale venues have dress codes.

culturalTips.title: City Rhythm
culturalTips.content: Shanghai is a 24-hour city. Shops often open late, and many restaurants take last orders at 9-10pm. Night markets come alive after 8pm.

culturalTips.title: Social Customs
culturalTips.content: Punctuality valued in business. Tipping not expected but appreciated in upscale establishments. Business cards exchanged with both hands.

culturalTips.title: Haipai Culture
culturalTips.content: Haipai (海派) refers to Shanghai's unique blend of Eastern and Western cultures. This cosmopolitan style influences everything from architecture to cuisine, characterized by openness, innovation, and elegance.

culturalTips.title: Shikumen Architecture
culturalTips.content: Shikumen (石库门) are historic townhouses with distinctive stone gate entrances that blend Chinese and Western elements. Many have been preserved in Xintiandi and Tianzifang as cultural heritage sites.

culturalTips.title: Qipao and Fashion
culturalTips.content: The iconic qipao (旗袍) was refined in Shanghai during the 1920s-30s. Today, Shanghai remains China's fashion capital with designers blending traditional elements with modern styles.

culturalTips.title: Nightlife Culture
culturalTips.content: Shanghai is China's nightlife capital with bars, clubs, and entertainment venues that stay open until dawn. Popular areas include Xintiandi, Former French Concession, and Jing'an.

culturalTips.title: Café Culture
culturalTips.content: Following European traditions, Shanghai has a strong café culture especially in the Former French Concession. Sunday brunch is a beloved local tradition with friends and family.

culturalTips.title: Arts Scene
culturalTips.content: Shanghai hosts world-class museums, galleries, and cultural venues. M50 and 1933 Old Mill Fun are creative hubs, while West Bund Cultural Center is the newest arts district.

culturalTips.title: Historical Layers
culturalTips.content: Shanghai evolved from a fishing village to a treaty port with international concessions, creating layers of history reflected in its diverse architecture from colonial mansions to modern skyscrapers.

culturalTips.title: Benbang Cuisine
culturalTips.content: Benbang (本帮) cuisine refers to traditional Shanghai-style cooking characterized by sweetness, careful use of soy sauce, and emphasis on fresh ingredients. Signature dishes include braised pork belly and hairy crabs.

culturalTips.title: Shanghainese Language
culturalTips.content: Shanghainese (上海话) is a Wu Chinese dialect quite different from Mandarin. While Mandarin is universally understood, locals appreciate visitors learning basic Shanghainese phrases.

culturalTips.title: Dining Etiquette
culturalTips.content: Chopsticks should not be stuck vertically in rice. Wait for the host to start eating. Turn the bowl when finished to show you've eaten. Tea is usually served automatically.

culturalTips.title: Metro Culture
culturalTips.content: Shanghai's metro is one of the world's largest and most efficient. During rush hours (8-9am, 6-7pm), expect crowded cars. Give up seats for elderly, pregnant women, and those with children.

culturalTips.title: Wu Dialect Family
culturalTips.content: Shanghainese belongs to the Wu dialect family, significantly different from Mandarin. Common phrases: 'ni' (你) = you, 'wo' (我) = I, 'na' (那) = that. Tone differences make it almost unintelligible to non-speakers.

culturalTips.title: Haipai Fashion
culturalTips.content: Shanghai pioneered the modern qipao (旗袍) in the 1920s-30s. Today, the city remains China's fashion capital, with Shanghai Fashion Week and numerous designers blending Eastern and Western styles.

culturalTips.title: Air Quality
culturalTips.content: Air quality can be poor, especially in winter. Check AQI before outdoor activities. Major international hotels have air purifiers. Masks recommended during heavy pollution.

culturalTips.title: Wu Dialect Family
culturalTips.content: Shanghainese belongs to the Wu dialect family, significantly different from Mandarin. Common phrases: 'ni' (你) = you, 'wo' (我) = I, 'na' (那) = that. Tone differences make it almost unintelligible to non-speakers.

culturalTips.title: Haipai Fashion
culturalTips.content: Shanghai pioneered the modern qipao (旗袍) in the 1920s-30s. Today, the city remains China's fashion capital, with Shanghai Fashion Week and numerous designers blending Eastern and Western styles.

culturalTips.title: Air Quality
culturalTips.content: Air quality can be poor, especially in winter. Check AQI before outdoor activities. Major international hotels have air purifiers. Masks recommended during heavy pollution.

culturalTips.title: Shanghai Greetings
culturalTips.content: While Mandarin 'ni hao' is universal, Shanghainese greeting 'nong hao' (侬好) means 'hello' in the local dialect. Locals appreciate when visitors try local phrases.

culturalTips.title: Dim Sum Culture
culturalTips.content: Shanghai dim sum differs from Cantonese style. Try local specialties like shengjian bao (pan-fried soup dumplings), xiaolongbao, and crab roe dishes.

culturalTips.title: Art Deco Legacy
culturalTips.content: Shanghai has one of the world's largest collections of Art Deco architecture, second only to Miami. Many buildings along the Bund feature this 1920s-30s style.

emergencyContacts.name: 上海和睦家医院
emergencyContacts.address: Shanghai Centre, 1376 Nanjing West Rd, Jing'an

emergencyContacts.name: 上海旅游咨询中心
emergencyContacts.notes: English service available. Tourist complaints, inquiries, emergency assistance.

emergencyContacts.name: 上海市消费者权益保护
emergencyContacts.notes: Consumer complaints, fraud reports, refund disputes

emergencyContacts.name: 上海市民服务热线
emergencyContacts.notes: Government services, non-emergency municipal issues, complaints

emergencyContacts.name: 英国驻上海总领事馆
emergencyContacts.notes: For UK citizens: 24h consular emergency line

emergencyContacts.name: 韩国驻上海总领事馆
emergencyContacts.notes: For Korean citizens: consular services and emergency assistance

emergencyContacts.name: 上海华山医院
emergencyContacts.notes: Top neurology and neurosurgery hospital in China

emergencyContacts.name: 旅游紧急求助
emergencyContacts.notes: 24h tourist emergency hotline for foreign visitors

quickFacts.visa: Most nationalities require visa (144h transit visa available at airport for eligible nationalities)
quickFacts.water: Tap water safe but recommended to use filtered or bottled
